title: Deployment modality declaration (v1alpha2)
description: Declares HOW an instance is deployed, explicitly DECOUPLED from WHICH edition it runs. TARGET-STATE contract. Modality (self_hosted / byoc / managed) and edition (personal / team / organization) are orthogonal axes: a Personal instance can be self-hosted OR byoc; a Team instance can be self-hosted, byoc OR managed. Mixing the two axes onto one dimension is a documented anti-pattern (see ADR-0010). This document carries NO secrets.

modality.description: How the infrastructure is run: self_hosted (owner runs everything), byoc (owner's cloud, Hub orchestrates), managed (Ironbat operates the infrastructure).

edition.description: Independent product edition. Any (modality, edition) pair is valid EXCEPT managed+personal (managed is a paid operational service that presupposes a Team/Organization subscription).

operator.description: Operator presence follows modality, not edition: managed -> required, byoc -> optional, self_hosted -> absent by design.

managed_by.description: Who operates the infrastructure. `ironbat` only for managed.

data_region.description: Explicitly chosen data-residency region (no silent default).
